title: “I do not see any other thing, monks, through which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states decline, as great desire. For one with…
content: “I do not see any other thing, monks, through which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states decline, as great desire. For one with great desire, monks, unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states decline.”

title: “Good, venerable sir, if the Blessed One would teach me the Dhamma in brief. Having heard the Dhamma from the Blessed One, I will dwell…
content: “Good, venerable sir, if the Blessed One would teach me the Dhamma in brief. Having heard the Dhamma from the Blessed One, I will dwell alone, secluded, heedful, ardent, and resolute.” - “Koṭṭhita, whatever is suffering — there you should abandon your desire. And what, Koṭṭhita, is suffering? The eye is suffering — there you should abandon your desire. Forms are suffering — there you should abandon your desire. Eye-consciousness is suffering — there you should abandon your desire. Eye-contact is suffering — there you should abandon your desire. Whatever feeling arises dependent on eye-contact — pleasant, painful, or neither-painful-nor-pleasant — that feeling is suffering. There you should abandon your desire.”

title: “‘Come, monk, guard the sense faculties. When you see a form with the eye, do not grasp the general feature, do not grasp the particular…
content: “‘Come, monk, guard the sense faculties. When you see a form with the eye, do not grasp the general feature, do not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the eye faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise — restrain that cause, guard the eye, practice restraint of the eye. When you hear a sound with the ear … smell an odor with the nose … taste a flavor with the tongue … feel a touch with the body … cognize a phenomenon with the mind, do not grasp the general feature, do not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the mind faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise — restrain that cause, guard the mind, practice restraint of the mind.’”

title: “If he feels a pleasant feeling, he understands: ‘It is impermanent.’ He understands: ‘I am not attached to it.’ He understands: ‘I do not delight…
content: “If he feels a pleasant feeling, he understands: ‘It is impermanent.’ He understands: ‘I am not attached to it.’ He understands: ‘I do not delight in it.’ If he feels a painful feeling … If he feels a neither-painful-nor-pleasant feeling, he understands: ‘It is impermanent.’ He understands: ‘I am not attached to it.’ He understands: ‘I do not delight in it.’ If he feels a pleasant feeling, he feels it as one who is not bound. If he feels a painful feeling, he feels it as one who is not bound. If he feels a neither-painful-nor-pleasant feeling, he feels it as one who is not bound. When he feels a feeling ending with the body, he understands: ‘I feel a feeling ending with the body.’ When he feels a feeling ending with life, he understands: ‘I feel a feeling ending with life.’ He understands: ‘With the breakup of the body, after death, everything that is felt, in which he no longer delights, will become cool right here.’”

title: “Monks, defilement is a disease; defilement is a boil; defilement is a dart. Therefore, monks, the Tathāgata dwells without defilement, free from the dart. Therefore,…
content: “Monks, defilement is a disease; defilement is a boil; defilement is a dart. Therefore, monks, the Tathāgata dwells without defilement, free from the dart. Therefore, monks, if any monk has a wish, let him wish to dwell without defilement, free from the dart. Do not conceive about the eye, do not conceive in the eye, do not conceive from the eye, do not conceive ‘the eye is mine.’ Do not conceive about forms … Do not conceive about eye-consciousness … Do not conceive about eye-contact … Whatever feeling arises dependent on eye-contact … do not conceive about that feeling, do not conceive in it, do not conceive from it, do not conceive ‘that feeling is mine.’ Not conceiving thus, he does not cling to anything in the world. Not clinging, he is not agitated. Not agitated, he personally attains Nibbāna. He knows: ‘Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’”

title: Elder Parāpariya: Most bodies, Full of pus and blood, Are made by skilled people Into beautifully painted boxes. Bitter as sweetness, Suffering bound by the…
content:
Elder Parāpariya:

Most bodies,
Full of pus and blood,
Are made by skilled people
Into beautifully painted boxes.

Bitter as sweetness,
Suffering bound by the agreeable —
Like a sharp sword smeared with honey —
The greedy person does not know.

One who lusts after female form,
Female taste, female touch,
One who is infatuated by female smell,
Meets many sufferings.

All the streams from women —
Five kinds flow in five places —
A person with energy and strength
Can block them.

One who has a goal, abiding in Dhamma,
Skilled, clear-seeing,
Rejoices in doing the duty
Connected with Dhamma and meaning.

Sunk in temporary benefits,
Abandon what is unbeneficial.
If one thinks of such a thing,
Not the thing to be done —
That one, not heedless,
Sees clearly.

If it is a good deed to do,
Rejoice in practicing the Dhamma.
Seize the doing immediately —
That is truly the highest happiness.

With high and low means,
Deceiving others,
Killing, causing sorrow,
Violent, overpowering others —

As a strong man carves wood,
Driving out a peg with a peg,
So the good person,
Drives out a sense-faculty with a sense-faculty.

Developing faith, energy, concentration,
Training mindfulness and wisdom,
Using five to conquer five,
Stainless, one goes forward.

That brahmin, having a goal,
Abiding in Dhamma,
Following the Buddha’s instruction,
Complete, with full strength —
That person enjoys peace.

title: Elder Nun Subhā: Here, people are lustful, Heedless, with defiled minds, They oppose each other as enemies, They struggle in disputes. Killing, binding, torturing, Losing…
content:
Elder Nun Subhā:

Here, people are lustful,
Heedless, with defiled minds,
They oppose each other as enemies,
They struggle in disputes.

Killing, binding, torturing,
Losing wealth, grieving, worrying —
Those who sink in sensual pleasures
Meet many calamities.

Why do my relatives,
Like my enemies,
Bind me into the circle of sensual pleasures?
Know that I have gone forth
Because I see that sensual pleasures are fearful.

Silver and gold cannot
Destroy the defilements.
Sensual pleasures are enemies,
Murderers, killers.
They are adversaries,
Darts, bonds.

Why do my relatives,
Like my enemies,
Bind me into the circle of sensual pleasures?
Know that I have gone forth —
Shaved my head, wearing the great robe.

Living on food
Accumulated by alms-round,
Robes made from discarded rags —
Living thus is suitable for me,
The basic life of homelessness.

Great sages have abandoned
Sensual desires, both divine and human.
Dwelling secure, liberated,
Unshaken, at ease.

Do not lead me to sensual desires —
There is no safety in sensual desires.
Sensual desires are enemies, murderers,
Producing suffering, like a heap of burning coals.

The path is dangerous, full of fear,
Full of suffering, with thorns —
The path of craving is rugged,
The path of delusion is vast.

Filled with terrifying calamities,
Sensual desires are like a snake’s head —
Fools delight in sensual desires,
Blind worldlings.

Beings sink in the muddy swamp —
Those who do not see the world,
They do not know
The end of birth and death.

People, because of sensual desires,
Go to evil destinations —
Most go that way,
Making themselves sick.

Thus, sensual desires produce enmity,
They burn, they defile.
Sensual desires belong to the body —
They bind people to death.

Sensual desires make one insane,
They cause disordered speech and mind,
They defile beings,
About to fall into Māra’s trap.

Sensual desires are endlessly perilous,
Full of suffering, a great poison —
Little sweetness, they create strife —
They wither the bright day.

Thus I decide —
I will not turn back because of sensual desires,
Which cause misfortune —
I will always delight in Nibbāna.

Fighting against sensual desires,
I wait, cool, calm —
I will live heedfully,
Destroying the fetters of sensual desire.

I follow that path
That the great sages have walked —
Sorrowless, stainless,
Safe, straight — the Eightfold Path.

See this Subhā,
Established in the true Dhamma —
A goldsmith’s daughter,
Attaining freedom from sensual desire,
Sitting in meditation,
At the root of a tree.

title: “Monks, just as in the last month of the rainy season, in autumn, when the rice has ripened, a cowherd tending cows would poke the…
content: “Monks, just as in the last month of the rainy season, in autumn, when the rice has ripened, a cowherd tending cows would poke the cows on this side and that with a stick, restraining and checking them. Why is that? Because the cowherd sees that otherwise there would be death, imprisonment, beating, or blame. Monks, even so, I saw the peril, the degradation, the defilement of unwholesome states, and I saw the escape, the benefit, the purification of wholesome states.”

title: “A person intoxicated by craving Throws himself into the stream Like a spider into its own web. But the wise cut it off, Having abandoned…
content:
“A person intoxicated by craving
Throws himself into the stream
Like a spider into its own web.
But the wise cut it off,
Having abandoned all suffering, they wander on without desire.”

title: “Bound with sinews and bones, Plastered with flesh and skin, This body is covered by skin — Truth is not seen as it is. Within,…
content:
“Bound with sinews and bones,
Plastered with flesh and skin,
This body is covered by skin —
Truth is not seen as it is.

Within, it is full of liver, bladder,
Heart, lungs,
Kidneys and spleen,
Nasal mucus, saliva, sweat, fat,
Blood, synovial fluid, bile, and lymph.

From these nine streams,
Impurity constantly flows —
From the eyes, discharge flows,
From the ears, discharge flows.

From the nostrils, mucus flows,
From the mouth, sometimes vomit —
Bile and phlegm flow —
From the body, sweat and dirt.

In the hollow of the head,
The brain is full.
The fool, led by ignorance,
Thinks it is beautiful.

When it lies dead, swollen,
Livid, discarded in a charnel ground,
Relatives no longer care.

Dogs, jackals, wolves, worms,
Crows, vultures, and other creatures eat it.”
